framework_name: ADX(14) trend-strength + EMA9/21/55 alignment (repository-defined SPEC framework)

rejection_reason: Lower SPEC alignment (SPEC uses EMA alignment, not +DI/-DI); 'transition' requires an arbitrary tolerance band Wilder did not define. Documented as secondary/future-research.

framework_name: EMA9/21/55 stack + slope (no ADX strength gate)
rejection_reason: Drops the ADX strength gate the SPEC requires; without it, directional 'strong' labels fire during ranges (false positives). Strictly dominated by the SPEC framework. Secondary/fallback.

Volatility has no direction: cannot map strong_up/strong_down, so canonical coverage is incomplete; adding a direction signal would be forbidden mixing. Adopted as the SECONDARY volatile/range split inside the primary.
Same directional gap as ATR percentile; cannot populate strong_up/strong_down. Secondary volatility feature only.
Directional gap; range-based estimators degrade at 5m (microstructure noise dominates the high-low range). Future-research / secondary.

Directional gap (cannot map strong_up/strong_down). Best causal profile in the volatility family (no percentile fit). Recommended candidate implementation for the primary's volatile/range split, but not a standalone primary.

framework_name: Dow Theory swing structure (HH/HL vs LH/LL)
rejection_reason: Next-best directional-capable framework, but lower causal_safety (pivot-confirmation lag; ZigZag/centered-window look-ahead trap), higher implementation complexity, and needs an auxiliary volatility feature for volatile/range. Documented as the leading SECONDARY / future-research framework.
Practitioner-derived (limited peer review); reference open-source implementation is look-ahead biased (uses future candles) and must be rewritten for causal use. Secondary/future-research.
High implementation complexity; subjective sub-events need data calibration; designed for daily bars (severe 5m noise); low SPEC alignment. Future-research on a higher timeframe.

HARD GATE: low causal_safety (Kim-smoothed default is non-causal; whole-data EM trap) and requires a train-only fit not performable this run (no data). Latent states need post-hoc mapping; K>=4 needed for the 5 regimes. Future-research, data-dependent.
HARD GATE: Viterbi default is non-causal; requires train-only fit (no data this run); poor directional discrimination at 5m. Future-research.
HARD GATE: whole-data fit trap, no causal forward analog, requires train-only fit (no data). No temporal structure (label churn); no native transition. Unsupervised exploratory candidate only.
HARD GATE: centroid fit on full data trap; requires train-only fit (no data); no probabilistic confidence; atheoretic regime count. Record clusters as experimental_regime_candidate only.
HARD GATE: very low SPEC alignment — classifies the trading ENVIRONMENT, not price direction; cannot map any canonical price regime. Phase 3 secondary annotation / no-trade filter.
HARD GATE: cannot map canonical price regimes (a time/environment filter only). Phase 3 no-trade filter annotation.
Cannot map directional regimes; blocked this run by absent volume/OI/taker data. Secondary confidence (volume_score) / future-research.
